extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout caught a four-rider breakaway (BAKELANTS, SCHÖNBERGER, THOMAS Benjamin, DOUBEY) in the final 50 meters to win the sprint finish.
tactical_patterns: BikeExchange-Jayco committed riders across three stages for GROENEWEGEN Dylan, revealing over-investment in positioning that yielded nothing.
Breakaway delayed tactical poker until final 600m when Peloton gap was already critical, allowing catch.
VAN AERT caught break in final 50m despite having to navigate LAPORTE, proving closing speed trumps positioning.
MEEUS Jordi lost photo finish despite sprinting faster from behind, showing momentum can't overcome late launch.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Extended GC lead via bonus seconds while winning Stage 5, demonstrating dual sprint-GC capability on selective finishes.
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: Failed to deliver despite three-stage team commitment from BikeExchange-Jayco, suggesting selective climbs neutralize pure sprinters.
rider_signals.BAKELANTS Jan: neutral: Showed willingness to work in break despite being former stage winner at Tour/Dauphiné, but poker tactics backfired.
rider_signals.MEEUS Jordi: positive: Matched VAN AERT in photo finish sprint, indicating emerging sprint threat on technical finishes.
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: neutral: Present in final sprint mix but unable to contend for win despite being well-positioned.
rider_signals.THOMAS Benjamin: neutral: Worked breakaway but tactical hesitation in final kilometer cost potential victory.
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: negative: Blocked VAN AERT's sprint line at 70km/h, revealing poor spatial awareness in leadout duties.
